item.title: Cain and Abel?
item.content:encoded: This and his brother are an interesting take on the Genesis account. So basically Abel might've been a sociopath (possibly sanctioned by SCP-343???) and Cain isn't proud of what happened between them? In any case, both get a +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't like this because I feel this embodies some of the bad traits that are stereotypical of Series I SCPs.

An invincible humanoid that is friendly and is allowed to freely roam the facility. Meanwhile the O5 Council are happy to give it free access to classified information for 'backup'? I'd love to hear the author's reasoning for that. Surely there are more sensible backup methods? What if the Chaos Insurgency or Serpent's Hand 'liberated' SCP-073? Kiss goodbye to all your secrets!

It's not to say that I totally dislike it, because the quality of writing and overall biblical idea is good. But because of the narrative given here, I'm going to have to give it -1. Not that it clearly matters anyway though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, this really doesn't strike me as much of a difficulty given his willingness to help. My first thought upon reading was, okay common sense approach, try to get him to do it himself.

This seems like something that probably should be updated, either with that happening or with a good reason for why it can't. I could see his curse as potentially preventing him from harming himself for instance. Can't have him escape by taking his own life.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A friend of mine from offsite pointed this out to me: isn't his weight way too low considering his height, and metal limbs? I don't think it's possible to weigh him without the limbs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've never found that to be a huge problem while writing them. I think it's more that people see enough power fantasies from other forms of media already, and they tend to come off as fairly blatant in the SCP format without any of the upsides that can come along with the genre.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"BeCaUsE iT'S a MarY sUE".

Many people don't like Humanoid scps that aren't forced to suffer through hell

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why? Sorry but i don't understand why you dislike them so much, i think they have there own unique qualities and i think they are different enough from the original source to be considered an original SCP. If you give more detail perhaps i could see your opinion a bit clearly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Can you elaborate of why you don't like those scips? if you don't mind me asking of course. and maybe some suggestions of what you could make it better?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Flush Able and Cain from the canon or form a Justice League and add some more random biblical and mythic immortals to it. I can't -1 enough.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What happens if Cain tries to harm himself? Like, what if someone gave him a needle and asked him to take his own blood sample?

Edit: oops, never mind. Probably should've read the other posts first.

item.title: I know, necropost
item.content:encoded: Then I would recommend changing it to "spine" or "spinal column

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hm, god point. Lemme think about it a bit

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wouldn't it make more sense to just put some sort of pressure-loaded spring by the door rather than assuming that a. the person doing the prank has authorization to talk to 073, let alone remove him from containment and b. 073 would be willing or interested in complying?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well I need to make him stand in a place a certain door will Hit him so somehow (yes my prank is that the person opening the door will Basically Hit themselves)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 073 doesn't seem like the type for pranks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: People, quick: What would be the perfect way to trick/blackmail 073 into doing something (not too harmful to him or others)? This is totally not connected to the great prank war in any way

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Mushrooms are not plants. For starter plants, as in Plantae kingdom classification, generally have to produce their own food through photosynthesis. Mushroom, as in organism in Fungi kingdom classification strictly obtains food from somewhere else, sometimes through plant symbiote such as in Lichen, or through fermenting organic materials such as in Natto and moldy bread.

Furthermore, plant cell walls are made of cellulose, compared to most fungi that have chitinous cell walls. The structure of mycellium found in fungal fruit bodies is also fairly different compared to structure of plant matters. Fungal reproduction generally doesn't have sexual system i.e. only one kind of cell exist that could merge with other cell to produce new individual, instead of two kind found in generative stage of most plants.

https://study.com/academy/lesson/the-evolution-of-plants-and-fungi-characteristics-evolutionary-history.html

item.title: mushrooms are plants?
item.content:encoded:
No, they're not.

EDIT: Is it ironic that this post is a necro?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm mostly sure mushrooms are plants

item.title: Re: About Cain's interactions with Able
item.content:encoded:
Hello!

It is okay to discuss old pages, but replying to old posts without adding anything meaningful to conversation is frowned upon, so you don't have to apologize.

The whole point of Cain and Able's relationship is that we don't know what happens. But making theories is fine, so…

+ Click to show
- Click to hide

I personally think that there are 4 possible outcomes for their encounter:

  1. Able kills himself on Cain. (Because of Cain's damage reflecting property)
  2. Able attacks Cain, both do not take damage. (Biblical Cain was cursed for killing Abel, if these two have a similar backstory, it would be fitting if hurting Cain didn't damage Able)
  3. Able attacks Cain, surrounding people take damage. (Since Cain said "it would be better for all parties involved" to keep them separate)
  4. They don't fight and talk instead. (You never know)

Actually, there is a story where Cain and Able meet, the joke tale Saturday Night SCP Showdown. Maybe there are more, I know only about this one.

but if Able were to attack Cain would he just die from Cain's ability? Wouldn't the foundation want to test this out to see if this would be a reliable way to take down Able?

+ Click to show
- Click to hide

I think you misunderstand something. If Cain's properties work on Able like on any human, then Able will take damage from hurting Cain, and if he dies from that, he'll just respawn in his coffin. Cain reflects damage, he doesn't kill anyone who hurts him.

So, if Cain's properties work on Able, then it won't be a reliable way to take him down, it will be the same as if someone else took a sword and cut Able with it. Except the someone else will be killed by a pissed off Able, while Cain is invulnerable.

I don't know if I can add that myself or if the original author has to and either way I am not confident enough to add to other peoples work.

+ Click to show
- Click to hide

You cannot add stuff to other people's work, sorry. You can add stuff like this to someone else's pages only if they are tagged as "collaboration". But, you can write that log as a tale, and it will be okay, as long as people will like it. Just read the guides and ask people for feedback in appropriate places, and you'll be fine.

item.title: About Cain's interactions with Able
item.content:encoded: Hi I am new here so I apologize for reviving an old thread. My main question is what would happen if Cain had to fight Able? Its clear that both would rather not interact but if Able were to attack Cain would he just die from Cain's ability? Wouldn't the foundation want to test this out to see if this would be a reliable way to take down Able? I feel like its could be an interesting doctor's log at least where a doctor asks Cain about it and he says some cryptic creepy stuff. I don't know if I can add that myself or if the original author has to and either way I am not confident enough to add to other peoples work. Thanks for anyone that reads this.

item.title: Please stop, for your own good and the sanity of others.
item.content:encoded:
srcs Hey! I can see you're getting back into the trend of posting a bunch of necro-post responses to people who aren't around to reply, and posting low-effort crap besides that.

I'm gonna lay it out for you. If you're not happy with a work, and you think it's something that should be removed, rewritten, reassessed, or voted against, then it helps to actually engage in substantial discussion on the merits or lack thereof on the work, and go beyond a simple knee-jerk bullshit justification to dismiss it.

Spamming little argumentative quips to year-plus-old posts is simply slinging pathetic pot-shots, at targets that aren't going to defend themselves, and is just a shitty, ineffective way to bring about change.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Subject is allowed to freely wander the facility and eat in the main canteen."

Downvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh. Ok.

I am STUPID.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-073's presence is inimical to any and all life grown in soil, causing death to any such life within a twenty (20) meter radius.

Pretty sure this didn't happen to the Biblical Jesus.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i love the fact hat he is pretty much just jesus

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: John Doe is a common name used for unidentified persons, not originally a roblox thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"John Doe"

Isn't that the name of a roblox hoax?

item.title: l
item.content:encoded: Really this guy just scares me because he's Cain but the evil one is Abel. In the bible, Cain killed Abel so does that mean 073… has been plotting this whole time!?

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
ETA: Also, if you have a suggestion on how multiple materials could be worked in here please let me know. That could be rather interesting.

Other SCPs that use beryllium bronze are:

SCP-1216: Organisms found in Massachusetts
SCP-2711 & SCP-2481: SCPs related to the Chinese interpretation of the Broken God and Xia Dynasty anomalous civilisation
SCP-1427: Semitic reference, as you have mentioned

I am thinking more of a note that identifies that beryllium bronze has been utilised in various methods by different anomalous cultures and entities. Perhaps the Foundation may attempt to trace an origin and how beryllium bronze was spread (and they can ask Cain about it, since he is sapient and more-or-less cooperative with the Foundation).

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Whoops, it was what I had copied from the other article. It's been fixed.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Cain's implants and SCP-2970's implants are definitely similar. It certainly is possible that they share a common origin. However, I think that our intent was to leave the LoE's identification with any specific mythical figures as ambiguous. Yet it is definitely interesting that one of those figures directly associated with The Lord of Endowments was the instigator in the biblical Cain's parents' fall from grace (or uplifting to sapience, depending on your point of view).

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Examination of the unidentified metal has suggested that it is beryllium bronze stele,

A stele is a free-standing upright slab or column. It doesn't make any sense in context here.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Oricha's Folly, to be specific.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: That's still something that could be looked into, my knowledge is fairly limited in that department so I appreciate the input. Thanks for bringing that up.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
As an aside, someone having the thought of writing an article on beryllium bronze as a whole?

SCP-148 and whatever that CotBG tale that explores the link between telekill, Mekhane and Wan.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The Apotheosis cycle has tons of references to Semitic religions and beryllium bronze. I'm not sure that any of it is super relevant, even if The Lord of Endowments is the Serpent of the Garden (which we left ambiguous on purpose), he was out of that story before Cain was born.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
The tie to 1427 was due to its Semitic references as well, not just because it is beryllium bronze. To my knowledge this is the only article with mentions or references to both of these things. I can try to find others though, or if anyone else knows of any they are free to suggest them. I'm also gonna edit my original post for clarity.

ETA: Also, if you have a suggestion on how multiple materials could be worked in here please let me know. That could be rather interesting.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Examination of the unidentified metal has suggested that it is beryllium bronze stele, however due to the nature of SCP-073 this has yet to be confirmed

Considering that there are many SCPs that have beryllium bronze in them (beyond 1427), would those other SCPs be accounted for too? Why 1427 specifically? If you list more of them, it might illustrate an aspect of the Foundation mythos rather than making an isolated one-to-one connection.

For a list of those other beryllium bronze SCPs, search them on the search bar.

As an aside, someone having the thought of writing an article on beryllium bronze as a whole?

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired: Edit made. This change can be reverted by the author at any point in time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 72 hours if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
- hide block

Due to the feedback received, this proposed edit has been changed since its initial posting. See the discussion below.

I propose the addition of the following addendum in order to touch on the points brought up during discussion:

Addendum 073-3: Examination of the unidentified metal on SCP-073 has suggested that it is beryllium bronze, a metal that has been documented as being utilized by various anomalous cultures and entities. Most notably, beryllium bronze is a component found in SCP-1216, SCP-1427, SCP-2481, and SCP-2711. In light of this discovery, the Foundation began working in an attempt to trace the origin of beryllium bronze and how it initially spread throughout the world. When prompted, SCP-073 was able to provide information that suggests that beryllium bronze originated in the Middle East, though the exact point of origin has yet to be determined. Further research into the origin of beryllium bronze is currently ongoing.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: Blood Sample
item.content:encoded: Would it be possible for 073 to extract his own blood for a blood sample? Additionally, what would be the result if one were to request "Blood of Cain" or "073's Blood" from SCP-294

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Changed "a" to "an" in "a eight-hundred page book". Like, really? How old is this article, and no one did that? Sorry, that sounds like I'm complaining, I'm just wondering how that happened.

item.title: Re: Dictionary?
item.content:encoded: It never said that the dictionary was in the form of a book. It could have been digital.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Cain, could you spit in this petri dish, please?"
Also he could cut some hair or nails by himself.

item.title: One Question Regarding SCP-073
item.content:encoded: First off, cleverly written. But I am wondering, is there a reason for the metal body parts? I do not recall seeing any mention about that in the ancient texts describing him.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I dunno. We actually have changed this article — recently! — to clarify one of the elements, and are likely to change it again for tone edits.

But the thing is, a free-roaming SCP isn't necessarily a bad thing. He is in fact contained. It's a risk, but there is always a risk, and what's an 'acceptable risk' is determined by who's in charge. There's no actual objective standard.

The Foundation backup thing doesn't initially make sense, but when you take the SCP in context of other world-ending and world-resetting anomalies… and the implication that the historical world Cain came from isn't quite the historical world we see from archaeological digs… and things like SCP-2000… well, that casts that element in a bit of a different light.

As a side note, this continues to be one of my favorite SCPs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Freeroaming SCP that can't be allowed near plant-based items, causes harm to things that hurt it (whether intentional or not), is being used as a backup by the Foundation despite the fact that they have more reliable means of data storage available (also ignores the "don't you put it in your mouth if you don't know where it's been" rule) and is From The Bible. I really enjoy the writing, and the powers are coolmore inconvenient for -073 than usefulbut allowing him full access to the site, let alone to all SCP information, feels a little weird. At 266, I doubt anything's going to change, especially given the historical value of this article, but it's something to look at, I guess.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As much as I like Cain, there are two things that have not aged well, and thus earn my downvote: First, he is a free-roaming humanoid SCP Object (within the bounds of the site, sure) and thus uncontained; second, he is being used by the Foundation as a data backup on all things SCP - so there may be unintended interactions with infohazards and memetic effects.

As great a take on the Biblical character that this is, I have to downvote. Site standards have evolved in the last few years, and Cain doesn't measure up anymore.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is digging into semantics, but if you look at this section of the description:

Violence directed towards SCP-073 reflects any damage inflicted on SCP-073 directly back onto the attacker, although SCP-073 visibly remains unharmed. This applies to any damage directed at SCP-073.

If that wall was thrown at 073, then it or the person who threw the wall at 073 would likely get damaged. If 073 stubbed its toe, I think it'd just get hurt because technically it's the one responsible, unless that wall moved towards the toe on its own. If someone without corresponding genitalia kicked 073 there, they'd probably feel pain comparable to what a normal male would have felt, noting:

personnel carrying out the action felt the sensation of whatever was applied to SCP-073

Some people have apparently looked into the question of whether childbirth or getting kicked in the nuts hurts more. Basically, it's comparable. From what I understand, 073 transfers damage; it doesn't have to be comparable so far as anatomical structures goes, but rather in magnitude and maybe general location.

I don't really see the paradox here. 073's ability wouldn't necessarily apply to self harm, as it would be its own aggressor, so the "damage" would be redirected to itself, essentially the same as would happen to a human who nicked themselves while shaving, or something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm just wondering, can he damage himself? Like if he stubs his toe by accident, does the wall get damaged? And what if a girl kicked him in the balls, because she doesn't have balls…

Also, if the wall gets damaged or something when he kicks it, after playing the RPG horror game Ib (look it up it's amazing) I realized a paradox. You see, in the game, there is a rose that is tied to your life, and if someone takes the petals off, you get hurt. So what if Cain took one of the petals off his own rose? It would circulate an unending paradox.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this SCiP, but its weird to think about how much hate this draft would get if it were submitted today.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Can anyone tell me if in the previous iterations of this document, SCP-073 could turn into a dog? Because right now I'm willing to upvote.

Looking in the history of this page, (thankfully) he didn't. Don't know where ya got that from.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Can anyone tell me if in the previous iterations of this document, SCP-073 could turn into a dog? Because right now I'm willing to upvote.

Anyway, I think that this document and the SCP-076 document aren't good stand-alone, but with the company of each other they form a strong, interesting narrative that is never actually mentioned.

item.title: Re: Dictionary?
item.content:encoded: Plastic paper?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This guy.

I love this guy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's the reason he doesn't actually get killed off. That's not a valid reason for why the attempt doesn't exist, otherwise the termination log wouldn't exist to begin with. In any case, it doesn't matter, upon looking at the revision history more closely it turns that Cain was in the termination logs at one point but was later deleted for w/e reason, I guess the entry wasn't interesting enough.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Because the attempt would inevitably fail? It's an unpopular topic because people continuously miss the point of 682, that nothing we try works.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know this is an unpopular topic but, why hasn't anyone tried using him against 682? The experiment with 053 failed mainly because 682 refused to attack her. Cain has similar "damage reflection" properties and is 682 is likely to be willing to attack him.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Kain Pathos Crow. I think.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know that there's a sort of air of "nooo these guys aren't Biblical why would they be Biblical", but ignoring all that, this is a fascinating interpretation of Cain's punishment. He was cursed to walk the earth as penance for his sin. It's never stated how long he was to walk. Perhaps, it was forever. This also ties in to the legend of the Wandering Jew, a similar story of a man cursed to walk the earth for all eternity. Does anyone know who the author is?
